name: Kochu Jang (Hot Sauce)
recipeInstructions:
- Combine Yeodkireum powder and water in a bowl. Mix well, then strain the liquid into a large pot.
- Add glutinous rice powder to the liquid and mix well. Cook over low heat (113°F, 45°C).
- Remove from heat and allow to stand until the glutinous rice powder is dissolved.
- Heat to boiling, then reduce heat and let cook for 30 minutes.
- Transfer to a large bowl to cool completely.
- Stir in Meju and chili powder, then blend well. Leave overnight.
The next day, mix in 4 cups of salt and transfer mixture to a large container. Sprinkle remaining salt over, then cover with loosely woven cloth (such as cheesecloth or gauze). Leave in a sunny place to ferment, stirring occasionally, for one month.
Available in Korean markets. Consists of soy beans made into dumplings, fermented, dried, and then powdered.
